title: Rich Silky Argentinian Walnut Empanadas
description: Salty and cozy Argentinian empanadas filled with walnuts and pickle, perfect for a snack or light meal. These empanadas are halal-friendly and feature a silky texture.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the flour, salt, and black pepper to make the empanada dough.
Gradually add the olive oil to the flour mixture and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
In a separate bowl, mix together the chopped walnuts, pickle, and onion.
Divide the empanada dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Roll out each ball of dough into a thin circle.
Place a tablespoon of the walnut mixture onto the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half-moon shape and press the edges together to seal.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with the beaten egg for a golden glaze.
Place the empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 20 minutes.
